title: Establish and Manage Cryptographic Keys
description: Establish and manage cryptographic keys for required cryptography employed in organizational systems.

checks.name: Key Management Policy Documented
checks.purpose: Verify the organization has a documented cryptographic key management policy covering generation, distribution, storage, access, retirement, and destruction — policy review only

checks.name: BitLocker Key Protector Uses TPM
checks.purpose: Verify BitLocker uses TPM as a key protector (TPM, TPM+PIN, or TPM+Recovery), ensuring encryption keys are protected by hardware and not stored in plaintext

checks.name: Key Rotation and Escrow Procedures Exist
checks.purpose: Verify documented procedures exist for cryptographic key rotation and secure escrow of recovery keys — policy review only

checks.name: Key Management Policy Documented
checks.purpose: Verify the organization has a documented key management policy — policy review only

checks.name: BitLocker Key Protector Uses TPM
checks.purpose: Verify BitLocker key protectors on the server use TPM-based protection to prevent plaintext key exposure

checks.name: Certificate Services Key Management Documented
checks.purpose: Verify PKI/certificate authority key management procedures are documented including CA key protection and renewal schedules — policy review only

checks.name: Key Rotation Procedures Exist
checks.purpose: Verify documented procedures for periodic key rotation and compromise response — policy review only

checks.name: Key Management Policy Documented
checks.purpose: Verify the organization has documented cryptographic key management procedures — policy review only

checks.name: LUKS Encrypted Volumes Have Key Slots Configured
checks.purpose: Verify LUKS encryption is in use with active key slots, confirming disk encryption keys are managed and properly configured

checks.name: No Private Key Files Are World-Readable
checks.purpose: Confirm no .key or .pem files have world-readable permissions, preventing unauthorized access to private cryptographic key material

checks.name: Key Management Policy Documented
checks.purpose: Verify the organization has documented cryptographic key management procedures — policy review only

checks.name: LUKS Encrypted Volumes Have Key Slots Configured
checks.purpose: Verify LUKS encryption is in use with active key slots, confirming disk encryption keys are managed and properly configured

checks.name: No Private Key Files Are World-Readable
checks.purpose: Confirm no .key or .pem files have world-readable permissions, preventing unauthorized access to private cryptographic key material

remediation: Document a cryptographic key management policy. Configure BitLocker with TPM+PIN key protectors and escrow recovery keys to Active Directory or a KMS. On Linux, ensure LUKS keys are properly configured and restrict permissions on private key files to 600 (owner read/write only).
